title: Track uses of "TODO" tags
description: The presence of TODO comments in code indicates unfinished work or technical debt. While these can be useful reminders, they should be tracked and resolved before production to avoid hidden issues and maintain code quality.
message: TODO comment found. Address or remove before production.
impact: Unresolved TODOs can lead to forgotten work, technical debt, and potential defects if left in production code.
recommendation: Regularly review and resolve TODO comments. Use issue tracking systems to manage technical debt and ensure all TODOs are addressed before release.

noncompliant.description: A TODO comment is left in the code.

compliant.description: The TODO has been addressed and removed.

devsecops.security: Unresolved TODOs may hide security-relevant work or indicate incomplete security controls.
devsecops.automation: Automated tools should flag TODOs so they are tracked and resolved.
devsecops.compliance: Leaving TODOs in production code may violate secure coding standards and audit requirements.

why_is_this_an_issue: TODO comments are meant for temporary reminders. If left unresolved, they can accumulate, leading to technical debt, missed requirements, or even security gaps.
how_to_fix: Review all TODO comments, create tickets for necessary work, and resolve or remove them before code is merged or released.

security_mappings.CERT: MSC00-J: Use comments judiciously and document assumptions
security_mappings.OWASP: A4: Insecure Design (if TODOs relate to security controls)
